IActiveDirectoryConfigurationInstanceCon.description:
Active directory configuration instance configuration. The machine will be used to configure and manage active directory configuration.
Accelerator can create users and groups when the following configuration is provided

adPerAccountGroups.description: Active directory per account group list

enableTerminationProtection.description: Flag for Ec2 instance enable api termination protection

IActiveDirectoryConfigurationInstanceUse.description:
User data scripts to create users, groups, password policy.

Accelerator can provision users, groups when following user data scripts are provided, these scripts are part of Accelerator sample configuration

scriptName.description: Friendly name for the user data script

IActiveDirectoryPasswordPolicyConfig.description: Managed active directory user password policy configuration

complexity.description: Whether password complexity requirements are enforced

failedAttempts.description: Number of failed login attempts before account lockout

history.description: Number of previous passwords to remember

lockoutAttemptsReset.description: Time in minutes after which failed login attempt counter is reset

reversible.description: Whether passwords can be stored using reversible encryption

principal.description: Type of IAM principal type like service, account, principalArn or provider, which can assume this role.

type.description:
IAM principal of either service, account, principalArn or provider type.

IAM principal of sns service type (i.e. new ServicePrincipal('sns.amazonaws.com')), which can assume this role.

name.description: Identity Center PermissionSet permissions boundary customer managed policy name.

path.description: The path to the IAM policy that you have configured in each account where you want to deploy your permission set.

name.description:
A name for the IAM group. For valid values, see the GroupName parameter for the CreateGroup action in the IAM API Reference. If you don't specify a name, AWS CloudFormation generates a unique physical ID and uses that ID for the group name.

If you specify a name, you must specify the CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM value to acknowledge your template's capabilities. For more information, see Acknowledging IAM Resources in AWS CloudFormation Templates.

policySets.description:
Policy set configuration.

To configure IAM policy named Default-Boundary-Policy with permission boundary defined in iam-policies/boundary-policy.json file, you need to provide following values for this parameter.

providers.description: SAML provider configuration To configure SAML configuration, you need to provide the following values for this parameter. Replace provider name and metadata document file. Document file must be in config repository

permissionSetName.description: Permission Set name that will be used for the Assignment.

principalId.description: PrincipalId that will be used for the Assignment

principalType.description: PrincipalType that will be used for the Assignment

IIdentityCenterAssignmentPrincipalConfig.description: Identity Center Permission Set Assignment Principal Configuration

name.description: A name for the Identity Center Configuration

description.description: A description string for the Permission Set

name.description: A name for the Identity Center Permission Set Configuration

policies.description: Policy Configuration for Customer Managed Permissions and AWS Managed Permissions

sessionDuration.description: A number value (in minutes). The length of time that the application user sessions are valid for in the ISO-8601 standard.

IIdentityCenterPoliciesConfig.description: Identity Center Permission Set Policy Configuration

acceleratorManaged.description: List of the names customer managed policies that would be attached to permission set.

awsManaged.description: List of AWS managed policies that would be attached to permission set.

customerManaged.description: List of the names and paths of the customer managed policies that would be attached to permission set.

inlinePolicy.description:
The inline policy that is attached to the permission set.

 {@link  <http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-sso-permissionset.html#cfn-sso-permissionset-inlinepolicy> InlinePolicy }  reference

permissionsBoundary.description: Specifies the configuration of the AWS managed or customer managed policy that you want to set as a permissions boundary.

activeDirectoryConfigurationInstance.description: (OPTIONAL) Active directory instance to configure active directory

description.description: Descriptive text that appears on the details page after the directory has been created.

dnsName.description: A fully qualified domain name. This name will resolve inside your VPC only. It does not need to be publicly resolvable.

edition.description: Active directory edition, example AWS Managed Microsoft AD is available in two editions: Standard and Enterprise

name.description: Friendly name for the active directory

netBiosDomainName.description: A short identifier for your Net BIOS domain name.

resolverRuleName.description: (OPTIONAL) Active directory route 53 resolver rule name

secretConfig.description:
(OPTIONAL) Active directory admin user secret configuration.

* {@link  IamConfig  }  /  {@link  ManagedActiveDirectoryConfig  }  /  {@link  ManagedActiveDirectorySecretConfig  }

sharedAccounts.description: (OPTIONAL) Active directory shared account name list.

sharedOrganizationalUnits.description:
(OPTIONAL) Active directory shared ou configuration.

* {@link  IamConfig  }  /  {@link  ManagedActiveDirectoryConfig  }  /  {@link  ManagedActiveDirectorySharedOuConfig  }

vpcSettings.description: Specifies the VPC settings of the Microsoft AD directory server in AWS

groupName.default: undefined, Accelerator will create log group name as /aws/directoryservice/DirectoryServiceName
groupName.description: Active directory log group name,  that will be used to receive the security logs from your domain controllers. We recommend pre-pending the name with /aws/directoryservice/, but that is not required.

IManagedActiveDirectorySecretConfig.description: Active directory admin user secret configuration.

account.description:
Active directory admin user secret account name. When no account name provided Accelerator will create the secret into the account MAD exists

Note: Please do not use the Management account for the admin user secret account name.

adminSecretName.description:
Active directory admin user secret account name. When no account name provided Accelerator will create the secret into the account MAD exists

Note: Please do not use the Management account for the admin user secret account name.

region.description: Active directory admin user secret region name. When no region name provided Accelerator will create the secret into the region MAD exists

IManagedActiveDirectoryVpcSettingsConfig.description: Specifies the VPC settings of the Microsoft AD directory server in AWS

subnets.description:
Friendly name of the vpc subnets, where active directory will be deployed

Minimum of two subnets from two different availability zone is required

vpcName.description: Friendly name of the vpc where active directory will be deployed

IPermissionsBoundaryConfig.description: Specify either customerManagedPolicy to use the name and path of a customer managed policy, or managedPolicy to use the ARN of an AWS managed policy.

awsManagedPolicyName.description: The AWS managed policy name that you want to attach to a permission set as a permissions boundary.

customerManagedPolicy.description: Specifies the name and path of a customer managed policy.

awsManaged.description: List of AWS managed policies. Values can be policy arn or policy name

IPolicyConfig.description: Use this configuration to define accelerator managed IAM managed policies.

name.description: The name of the managed policy.

policy.description: A JSON file containing policy boundary definition.

identityCenterDependency.description: Flag indicates if the policy is used in Identity Center PermissionSet assignments.

externalIds.description: List of IDs that the role assumer needs to provide one of when assuming this role

instanceProfile.description: Indicates whether role is used for EC2 instance profile

policies.description: List of policies for the role

metadataDocument.description: SAML metadata document XML file, this file must be present in config repository

name.description:
The name of the provider to create.

This parameter allows a string of characters consisting of upper and lowercase alphanumeric characters with no spaces. You can also include any of the following characters: _+=,.@-

Length must be between 1 and 128 characters.

boundaryPolicy.description:
AWS supports permissions boundaries for IAM entities (users or roles). A permissions boundary is an advanced feature for using a managed policy to set the maximum permissions that an identity-based policy can grant to an IAM entity. An entity's permissions boundary allows it to perform only the actions that are allowed by both its identity-based policies and its permissions boundaries.

Permission boundary is derived from iam-policies/boundary-policy.json file in config repository

disableConsoleAccess.description: A boolean value to define if the user should have access to the AWS console. True will disable console access, False will enable it. defaults to False.

group.description: Group to add this user to.

username.description:
A name for the IAM user. For valid values, see the UserName parameter for the CreateUser action in the IAM API Reference. If you don't specify a name, AWS CloudFormation generates a unique physical ID and uses that ID for the user name.

If you specify a name, you cannot perform updates that require replacement of this resource. You can perform updates that require no or some interruption. If you must replace the resource, specify a new name.

IUserSetConfig.description:
User set configuration

```
userSets:
  - deploymentTargets:
      accounts:
        - Management
    users:
      - username: accelerator-user
        boundaryPolicy: Default-Boundary-Policy
        group: Admins
```

NonEmptyString.description: A string that has at least 1 character
